summary: An agent's context window is not a free resource — past a fill threshold quality degrades silently, so context discipline (thresholds, persistence to the filesystem, fresh context per phase, cutting instead of steering) is as important as code discipline.

rules.rule: Never let the context window be the only place an important decision lives — anything that must survive the session goes to the filesystem (decision store, append-only work log, handoff artifact), and before cutting, verify a fresh agent could continue from the repo alone.

On detecting a qualitative degradation signal, the agent reports it explicitly to the human, does not proceed until the human confirms continue-or-cut, and records the warning in the active work-log if told to continue.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Past the red context threshold, do not start new complex work and never take major architectural decisions — close the current task, persist state, and cut the session.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: When an early decision turns out wrong, do not steer the agent back within the same context — persist the lesson, cut the session, and restart fresh with only the persisted artifacts.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: When a session ends, run the context-specific closeout checks: persist undocumented decisions, commit or explicitly discard uncommitted work, clean or declare worktrees, capture pending tasks, and update the operational-status doc if it drifts from reality.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never import external 'expert' skills onto work that touches product identity, vocabulary, architecture, data model, or UX; if any skill's output contradicts canon, canon wins and the agent reports the contradiction rather than reconciling the two.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
